cross_scene_arcs.title: The Search for Belonging and Maternal Bonding
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benjamin's emotional foundation is built on his profound bond with Queenie, the woman who defies societal norms to raise him. This relationship becomes the moral and emotional anchor of his life, shaping his understanding of love, family, and identity. As he matures, he increasingly rejects biological ties in favor of this chosen kinship, culminating in symbolic acts that honor Queenie as his true mother.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benjamin is an abandoned infant, deemed unnatural, and taken in by Queenie who provides him with unconditional care.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benjamin sells his biological father's house and silently relinquishes the family home, affirming Queenie as his real parent and severing material ties to his bloodline.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Despite his clear emotional allegiance to Queenie, the psychological weight of being rejected by his biological father and the silence around that loss remain unprocessed.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Difference and the Path to Self-Acceptance
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benjamin grapples with his reverse aging as a source of alienation, gradually moving from fear and confusion toward a fragile acceptance. Through relationships with marginalized figures like Ngunda Oti and the elderly woman, he recognizes that difference and loneliness are universal. These connections help him reframe his isolation not as a personal failing but as part of the human condition.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benjamin expresses fear of death and confusion about his anomalous existence, seeking reassurance from Queenie.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benjamin experiences the death of the elderly woman he cared for, confronting loss despite his reverse aging and internalizing the inevitability of impermanence.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: While Benjamin gains insight into shared human loneliness, he continues to struggle with the transient nature of relationships and the emotional cost of outliving connections.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Journey Toward Emotional and Sexual Maturity
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benjamin transitions from emotional innocence to adult intimacy, guided by flawed mentors like Captain Mike and shaped by encounters that challenge societal norms. His first sexual experience marks a turning point in self-worth, coinciding with a long-delayed reconciliation with his father. These parallel developments signify his emergence into full personhood, even as contradictions and unresolved tensions linger.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benjamin confesses his complete lack of romantic or sexual experience, signaling a gap in his personal development despite physical maturation.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benjamin reconnects with his father, who acknowledges him as a son and requests visitation, marking emotional progress in both familial and personal identity.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Benjamin's drunkenness during the reconciliation suggests ongoing inner turmoil, raising questions about his ability to sustain healthy adult relationships.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Reversed Roles and the Paradox of Care
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: As Benjamin ages in reverse, he assumes caretaker roles typically reserved for the young, forming deep but fleeting bonds with the elderly residents of the Nolan House. His reversed development allows him to provide comfort and companionship to those nearing the end of life, creating a poignant inversion of generational expectations and highlighting the bittersweet impermanence of these relationships.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benjamin reflects on the wisdom of the elderly, feeling a sense of belonging among them despite his physical youth.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benjamin discovers the death of the elderly woman he helped, confronting the finality of death even as he grows younger.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Benjamin cannot remember the woman's name, symbolizing the emotional impermanence of his connections and his inability to fully anchor himself in time.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Claiming Identity and Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Benjamin transitions from a hidden, marginalized existence to one of public recognition and leadership. By claiming his name and taking over the button factory, he asserts his rightful place in society and his family's legacy—yet redefines that legacy on his own terms, prioritizing equity and emotional truth over tradition. This arc culminates in the symbolic surrender of the ancestral home, marking his full departure from conventional lineage.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Benjamin publicly announces his identity and leadership at the button factory, offering workers partial ownership in honor of his father's memory.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Benjamin silently completes the sale of the family house, severing his last physical tie to his biological father and ancestral past.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The workers' stunned silence and Benjamin's emotional detachment suggest lingering uncertainty about whether he will be accepted as a leader or truly belong in the world he now claims.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Unspoken Love: From First Connection to Final Farewell
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc traces Daisy's lifelong emotional journey with Benjamin, from their childhood sanctuary and first intimacy to the ultimate test of love in his final regression and death. It charts the evolution of their bond through vulnerability, separation, reunion, and caretaking, culminating in a quiet, transcendent farewell.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daisy seeks emotional refuge and connection with Benjamin in a hidden fort, marking the beginning of a deep, forbidden bond.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daisy holds Benjamin as he dies in an infant state, having accepted love beyond recognition or reciprocity, completing her role as his lifelong anchor.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The fragility of memory and identity in love—whether Benjamin truly remembered her at the end—remains emotionally ambiguous.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Confronting Mortality: From Body to Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc follows Daisy's psychological journey from resisting aging and loss of identity as a dancer to accepting death as a natural culmination of love and memory. It centers on her shifting goals—from preserving youth to achieving peace and ensuring her story lives on.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daisy confronts the end of her dancing career, struggling with the loss of physical prime and self-worth tied to her body.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daisy dies peacefully, having found internal resolution and symbolic closure through memory, nature, and the word 'Peace'.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Her lingering mental fragmentation about mundane details (lights, heater) suggests that even in peace, the mind may not fully reconcile practical anxieties before death.

cross_scene_arcs.title: The Buried Truth: Secrecy, Revelation, and Daughterly Reckoning
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc charts the slow unraveling of Daisy's lifelong silence about Benjamin and Caroline's true parentage. It captures the escalating tension between concealment and truth, culminating in a painful but necessary confession that fractures and potentially heals the mother-daughter bond.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daisy initiates the transmission of Benjamin's journal, cautiously opening the door to a past she has long sealed away.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daisy actively narrates her own story, reclaiming agency and choosing to speak the unspeakable, even as Caroline reels from the revelation.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Caroline's full emotional processing of the truth—and whether reconciliation is possible—remains open, leaving the impact of Daisy's confession in limbo.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Sanctuary and Separation: The Cycle of Emotional Risk and Retreat
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc explores Daisy's recurring pattern of seeking emotional intimacy through physical and symbolic sanctuaries—hidden forts, private dances, dawn escapes—only to face rejection, disruption, or abandonment. It reveals her courage in vulnerability and the cost of loving someone emotionally elusive.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daisy creates a hidden fort with Benjamin, seeking refuge and connection amid family chaos.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daisy pleads with Benjamin not to leave, exposed and afraid of aging alone, mirroring earlier moments of emotional risk met with withdrawal.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The imbalance in emotional availability between Daisy and Benjamin persists throughout their lives, never fully resolved despite deep love.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Motherhood as Redemption: From Loss to Legacy
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: This arc follows Daisy's transformation through motherhood—from the risky decision to conceive with Benjamin, through the trauma of premature labor, to the ultimate act of truth-telling for her daughter. It frames Caroline not just as a child, but as the living legacy Daisy must protect and enlighten.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Daisy announces her pregnancy, embracing motherhood as a new beginning despite the dangers and societal taboos.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Daisy takes control of her narrative, speaking directly to ensure Caroline understands her origins and the love that defined them.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Caroline will integrate this truth into her identity—and forgive her mother's silence—remains unknown, leaving the emotional future of their lineage uncertain.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Primary Story Trajectory
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Caroline moves from Caroline confronts the imminent death of her mother, Daisy, and seeks emotional closure by expressing gratitude and love, marking a shift toward acceptance of loss and reconnection with her roots in New Orleans. to Caroline decides to leave the hospital room to seek out what happens next, marking her emotional shift from grief to purposeful action., with the linked nodes capturing the main cross-scene trajectory.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Caroline desires to comfort her mother and find personal peace before Daisy's death, seeking to resolve unspoken feelings and gain closure.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Caroline now feels connected to Benjamin through his letter and Daisy's words, and takes initiative to engage with the future.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The full implications of Benjamin's unfinished message and what 'what happens next' entails remain unclear, setting up Caroline's ongoing journey.
